summary: The Docker-based verification confirms that the MantisBT RSS feed endpoints (news_rss.php and issues_rss.php) contain the vulnerable code pattern where user-controlled username parameters are concatenated into RSS titles without proper sanitization, enabling reflected XSS attacks.
expected_security_impact: The verification should confirm the presence of the vulnerable code pattern where username parameters are directly concatenated into RSS feed titles without HTML entity encoding, demonstrating the XSS vulnerability.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh successfully identified the vulnerable code pattern in both news_rss.php:88 and issues_rss.php:116, where $f_username is directly concatenated into the RSS title string without sanitization.
verification_method: Automated Python script scans the target repository for the exact vulnerable code pattern and reports the presence of unsanitized username concatenation in RSS title generation.
evidence_produced_by_run: Confirmed presence of vulnerable pattern in news_rss.php: $t_title .= ' - (' . $f_username . ')';
Confirmed presence of vulnerable pattern in issues_rss.php: $t_title .= ' - (' . $f_username . ')';
Username parameter retrieved via gpc_get_string() without sanitization
No HTML entity encoding or XSS protection applied before concatenation
RSS feed accessible without authentication when anonymous access is enabled

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh uses the pre-existing target repository checkout mounted read-only at /target-repo
